dashboard.title: Your dashboard
dashboard.body: This is your home base — agent status, workspaces, and recent activity at a glance. Three quick steps get you running.

install.title: Step 1 · Install an agent
install.body: Open the Marketplace, pick an agent, and click Install.

agents.title: Step 2 · Create & test
agents.body: In Agents, create a new agent, enter its API key, click Test to verify, then Connect.

workspaces.title: Step 3 · Open a workspace
workspaces.body: Once connected, open a workspace from Workspaces and you're ready to go.

toast.fillRequiredFields: Fill in the required fields first
toast.loginTerminalOpened: Login terminal opened. Complete login there.
toast.enterWorkspaceName: Enter a workspace name, or skip this step

welcome.title: Welcome to OpenAgents Launcher
welcome.subtitle: Run, configure, and orchestrate AI coding agents from one place.

benefits.credentials: Manage API keys and credentials in one encrypted store
benefits.workspaces: Spin up workspaces and chat with running agents
benefits.connect: Connect to GitHub, Slack, Discord, Linear and more

welcome.footnote: We'll have you running your first agent in under two minutes.

agentSelection.title: Pick your first agent
agentSelection.subtitle: Only agents your installed runtime can run are shown. You can install more later from the Install tab.
agentSelection.installingDetail: First-time installs can take a few minutes — downloading the runtime and dependencies.
agentSelection.stillInstalling: The agent runtime is still installing — this can take a minute on first launch.

apiKey.title: Configure agent
apiKey.subtitleEnv: Configure {{label}}. Saved to ~/.openagents/env/ and injected into the agent automatically.
apiKey.subtitleLoginWithKey: {{label}} signs in through its own CLI — or enter an API key below instead.
apiKey.subtitleLogin: {{label}} signs in through its own CLI.
apiKey.whereKey: Where do I get a key?
apiKey.notInstalledHint: — install it first, then log in.
apiKey.installedNotSignedIn: {{label}} is installed — not signed in yet.
apiKey.loginInstructionsPrefix: {{label}} signs in through its own CLI. Open a terminal and run
apiKey.loginInstructionsMid: to authenticate. When the CLI says you're signed in, come back and click
apiKey.windowsShellNotePrefix: Claude Code on Windows needs PowerShell (opened for you) or Git for Windows. If login fails, install
apiKey.detectionNote: We can't reliably detect every CLI login, so this step never blocks you — if the agent isn't actually signed in, the Launch step will surface it.
apiKey.preferApiKey: Prefer an API key? Enter it instead:

workspace.title: Create a workspace
workspace.subtitle: A workspace is where agents collaborate. This is optional — you can skip it and connect one later.
workspace.hintPrefix: We'll create a new workspace at
workspace.hintMid: and bind your first agent to it. Prefer to do this later? Use

launch.title: Launch your agent
launch.subtitle: Start {{agentName}} and verify it reaches a healthy state.

wizard.title: Set up {{label}}
wizard.subtitle: Quick setup — configure, verify, then create your first agent.

apiConfig.noKeyRequired: This agent has no API key requirements. You can continue and create your first instance.
apiConfig.savedLocallySuffix: . Secrets are never printed to logs.

connectionTest.okHint: Connection looks good. Pick a name for your first agent instance.
connectionTest.failHint: You can go back and adjust the configuration, or skip the test.

createInstance.hint: Used as the local identifier — you can rename or remove it later from the Agents tab.
